cards.name: Join Zsolt Jozsef Simon — Porcelain Casting Workshop at Art & Design Building
cards.blurb: Grab something sweet at Swana Sweets while you're out on the Student Union patio and soak in the mild morning air.
cards.why: Perfect way to kick off a campus-centered day.

cards.name: Introduction to Python and Pandas at King Library
cards.blurb: Drop into this free Python and Pandas intro at King Library. No laptop? Just show up and see what it's about.
cards.why: Free, practical, and you'll actually walk away knowing something new.

cards.name: R&R, 2026: Recovering Ricardo Favela’s Royal Chicano Air Force Poster Collection at San José State University at King Library
cards.blurb: Browse the R&R, 2026 exhibition at King Library, a showcase of recovered posters from Ricardo Favela's Royal Chicano Air Force collection. Local history with serious visual punch.
cards.why: It's free art with real cultural weight right there on campus.

cards.name: Be Ready: Be Prepared for Disasters
cards.blurb: Swing by this disaster preparedness talk and actually get some useful info on what to do when things go sideways in the South Bay.
cards.why: Free and genuinely useful knowledge you'll be glad you have.

cards.name: Natalie and James Thompson Gallery: Click on My Mouth: Language and Technology at ART 127: Thompson Gallery
cards.blurb: Check out Click on My Mouth: Language and Technology at the Thompson Gallery. It's a thought-provoking show exploring how tech shapes the way we communicate.
cards.why: It's the kind of free art show that actually gives you something to talk about over dinner.

cards.name: San Jose Giants vs. Visalia Rawhide
cards.address: 588 E Alma Ave, San Jose
cards.blurb: Head to the ballpark for San Jose Giants vs. Visalia Rawhide. Get there early for decent seats, grab a beer and some ballpark food, and settle in for nine innings under the lights.
cards.why: A Thursday night baseball game on a 72-degree day is hard to beat.

cards.name: Anthony Jeselnik
cards.address: 62 S Second St
cards.blurb: If the game wraps up early or you're in the mood to keep the night going, Anthony Jeselnik is performing at 62 S Second St tonight. Dark humor, sharp delivery, zero chill.
cards.why: He's one of the best working comics right now and he's right here tonight.

cards.name: Spark60: Meditations for Mortals: The Liberating Art of Embracing Your Limits with Oliver Burkeman
cards.blurb: Catch Oliver Burkeman talk about embracing limits and doing less — it's a surprisingly refreshing way to kick off a Thursday morning.
cards.why: Free, thoughtful, and a genuinely good excuse to be out early.

cards.name: Market Street Spring Jubilee
cards.blurb: Hit the Market Street Spring Jubilee for live music and the general vibe of a street fest on a warm afternoon.
cards.why: 70 degrees and live music outside is pretty much the ideal Thursday.

cards.name: Jessica Monette: Root Me in the Soil, In the Wake and the Becoming (Project Room Exhibition)
cards.blurb: Wander through Jessica Monette's project room exhibition at SCU — soil, roots, and becoming, all in one quiet gallery space.
cards.why: It's free art you can actually stand in front of and think about.

cards.name: AI-Powered tools to help with your searching
cards.blurb: Drop into this free session on AI-powered search tools — practical, fast, and you'll probably leave knowing something useful.
cards.why: It's the kind of low-key skill upgrade that actually sticks.

cards.name: Anthony Jeselnik
cards.address: 62 S Second St
cards.blurb: Anthony Jeselnik live at 62 S Second St in San Jose. Dark, sharp comedy that does not pull punches.
cards.why: He's genuinely one of the best working stand-ups right now.

cards.name: Ethics Certificate Training for Students: April 9 & 16
cards.blurb: Sit in on the Ethics Certificate Training at SCU — even if you're not a student, the conversations in these sessions tend to be worth it.
cards.why: Free, thought-provoking, and a good way to fill the early evening.

cards.name: Chicago Presidential Reception
cards.blurb: Swing by the Chicago Presidential Reception at SCU — free event, good energy, and a solid reason to put on real clothes.
cards.why: You don't need a reason beyond free food and interesting people.

cards.name: Vibe & Thrive Mini Health Fair at Science Lawn
cards.blurb: Check out the Vibe & Thrive Mini Health Fair on the Science Lawn — early bird hours mean shorter lines and a chill vibe to ease into the morning.
cards.why: It's happening right now and it's free, so why not kick off the day with something different.

cards.name: Spark60: Meditations for Mortals: The Liberating Art of Embracing Your Limits with Oliver Burkeman
cards.blurb: Catch Oliver Burkeman's Spark60 talk on embracing your limits. It's a lecture-style format, so grab a seat and actually sit with the ideas he's throwing out.
cards.why: A talk about doing less and living better hits different first thing in the morning.

cards.name: Market Street Spring Jubilee
cards.blurb: Head to the Market Street Spring Jubilee for live music and the general good energy of a street festival on a 72-degree afternoon.
cards.why: Live music outside in perfect weather is hard to beat on a Thursday.

cards.name: Jessica Monette: Root Me in the Soil, In the Wake and the Becoming (Project Room Exhibition)
cards.blurb: Walk through Jessica Monette's Project Room Exhibition, Root Me in the Soil, In the Wake and the Becoming. Take your time with it; the work rewards slow looking.
cards.why: Free art that's actually worth the stop, not just filler.

cards.name: Be Ready: Be Prepared for Disasters
cards.blurb: Pop into the Be Ready: Be Prepared for Disasters community session. It's practical, genuinely useful info, and worth having if you live in the Bay Area.
cards.why: You've been meaning to actually learn this stuff, and now there's no excuse.

cards.name: Anthony Jeselnik
cards.address: 62 S Second St
cards.blurb: Anthony Jeselnik live at 62 S Second St. He's sharp, dark, and doesn't hold back, so go in knowing exactly what you're signing up for.
cards.why: Stand-up comedy on a Thursday night is the right call.

cards.name: GIS & Drone Society Club Meeting
cards.blurb: Swing by the GIS & Drone Society Club Meeting if you're at all curious about mapping tech or drones. It's low-key and open.
cards.why: Good way to fill the early evening with something genuinely nerdy and fun.

cards.name: Homework Help for Grades K-6
cards.blurb: Join the Qigong class at Cupertino Library — a low-key, grounding way to ease into the day with some gentle movement.
cards.why: Starting the morning with intentional movement sets a great tone for the rest of the day.

cards.name: Main Street Cupertino
cards.address: 19401 Stevens Creek Blvd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Main Street Cupertino and grab lunch at Lazy Dog or one of the Asian spots along the plaza — walk the outdoor space after and enjoy the afternoon air.
cards.why: It's right in Cupertino and the patio vibes are perfect for a mild April afternoon.

cards.name: AI-Powered tools to help with your searching
cards.blurb: Catch this free talk at Santa Clara University on AI-powered search tools — practical, interesting, and worth knowing about even if you're not a tech person.
cards.why: Free, genuinely useful info and a good way to spend an hour and a half after lunch.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Wander through the Triton Museum of Art — check out the contemporary galleries and spend some time in the sculpture garden before they close.
cards.why: Free world-class art that most people in the South Bay have never been to. Worth it every time.

cards.name: Bakenstein
cards.address: 1370 Homestead Rd, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Stop into Bakenstein for something sweet before the evening winds up — rated 4.9 and worth every bite.
cards.why: A pre-meditation treat from one of the highest-rated spots in Santa Clara is a solid call.

cards.name: Thursday Evening Meditation and Mindfulness
cards.blurb: End the day at Thursday Evening Meditation and Mindfulness in Cupertino. Drop in, decompress, and let the week melt off.
cards.why: Bookending a Qigong morning with an evening meditation session is a genuinely great Thursday.

cards.name: Spotlight Tours Thursdays | Anderson Collection
cards.address: 314 Lomita Dr, Stanford, CA 94305
cards.blurb: Join the Spotlight Tour at the Anderson Collection and let a docent walk you through the highlights of one of the best collections of postwar American art in the country.
cards.why: Free, no tickets needed, and a great warm-up before you come back to draw later.

cards.name: 1,000 Ways to Hold: A New Work By Erika Chong Shuch
cards.address: 314 Lomita Dr, Stanford, CA 94305
cards.blurb: Catch this new performance piece by Erika Chong Shuch at the Bing Studio — it's the kind of intimate, experimental work that's worth seeing without knowing too much going in.
cards.why: Free and it's happening right now on campus, so just go.

cards.name: Graphic Novel Book Club
cards.blurb: Head to the Graphic Novel Book Club. Even if you didn't read the book, these things are usually pretty welcoming and the conversation gets good fast.
cards.why: A free, low-key social hour that breaks up the visual art heavy evening nicely.

cards.name: Friends of The Library Membership Month Book Sale - Friends Preview Sale (w/ Annual Meeting)
cards.blurb: Browse the Friends Preview Sale before it opens to the general public — this is the good shift where you actually find stuff worth buying.
cards.why: Free books, good crowd, and you get first pick.

cards.name: Bloomsgiving
cards.address: 301 Castro St, Mountain View, CA 94041, USA
cards.blurb: Grab dinner here on Castro St — it's the kind of spot that feels right for a Thursday evening when you want something good without a whole production.
cards.why: Solid food, chill vibe, and they close at 8 so you're getting in at the right time.

cards.name: Pelicans, Herons, and Egrets, Oh My!
cards.blurb: Head to this free talk about pelicans, herons, and egrets in Los Altos. Whether you're a birder or just Bay-Area-curious, it's a genuinely interesting way to spend an evening.
cards.why: Free, educational, and way more fun than it sounds.

cards.name: Toddler Time
cards.blurb: Catch Read Me, the arts event in Mountain View. Show up, take it in, and see what's on the walls or stage tonight.
cards.why: It's a locked item for a reason — local arts nights like this are worth the effort.

cards.name: Books Inc.
cards.address: 317 Castro St, Mountain View, CA 94041, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down with a quick stop at Books Inc. on Castro. Browse whatever's on the staff picks table and grab something to read this weekend.
cards.why: Open until 10 and it's the perfect low-key cap to the night.

cards.name: Drop-In Drawing @ The Anderson
cards.address: 314 Lomita Dr, Stanford, CA 94305
cards.blurb: Drop in and draw at the Anderson Collection — no prep needed, just show up and sketch in one of Stanford's best art spaces.
cards.why: Free, low-key, and a genuinely nice way to spend a Thursday morning.

cards.name: Cantor Arts Center
cards.address: Cantor Arts Center, Stanford University
cards.blurb: Walk the Rodin sculpture garden, then head inside to browse the permanent collection and whatever's currently on view. It's free and genuinely world-class.
cards.why: You're already on campus and this is one of the best free museums in the Bay Area.

cards.name: Lou Henry Hoover Carillon Performance
cards.address: 550 Jane Stanford Way, Stanford, CA 94305
cards.blurb: Catch the Lou Henry Hoover Carillon performance on campus. It's a short stop but the bells filling the Stanford air is a pretty surreal midday moment.
cards.why: It's happening today and it costs almost nothing to swing by.

cards.name: Spotlight Tours Thursdays | Anderson Collection
cards.address: 314 Lomita Dr, Stanford, CA 94305
cards.blurb: Join the Thursday Spotlight Tour at the Anderson Collection and get a guided look at the pieces you just walked past on your own.
cards.why: Having a docent break down the collection makes it stick in a way solo browsing doesn't.

cards.name: The Dish Trail
cards.address: Stanford Foothills (Stanford Ave trailhead)
cards.blurb: Head up the Dish Trail for the late afternoon loop. The light at this hour is great and you'll get those wide foothill views looking out over the Bay.
cards.why: 72°F and sunny is basically the ideal Dish weather.

cards.name: Graphic Novel Book Club
cards.blurb: Wind down the evening at the Graphic Novel Book Club. Even if you haven't read this month's pick, it's the kind of crowd where that's fine.
cards.why: Good conversation with people who take comics seriously is a solid Thursday night.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Hit up a handful of the vendors, grab a coffee to start, then settle into tacos or banh mi on the patio before the lunch rush gets real.
cards.why: Best way to ease into the day with zero commitment and great food.

cards.name: San Jose Municipal Rose Garden
cards.address: Municipal Rose Garden
cards.blurb: Walk the grounds and take in the spring blooms — April is peak season and the garden looks genuinely stunning right now.
cards.why: It's free, it's beautiful, and 72°F is perfect for a slow stroll.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Work through the contemporary galleries at your own pace, then spend some time out in the sculpture garden before they close.
cards.why: Free world-class art with a sculpture garden is hard to beat on a Thursday afternoon.

cards.name: Santana Row
cards.address: 377 Santana Row, San Jose, CA 95128, USA
cards.blurb: Wander the boutiques along Santana Row, then grab a table at Straits or the Zinnia patio for dinner before the evening kicks off.
cards.why: It's the kind of place that feels effortlessly good on a mild spring evening.

cards.name: Anthony Jeselnik
cards.address: 62 S Second St
cards.blurb: Anthony Jeselnik is one of the sharpest, darkest stand-up comics working right now. Show up, sit back, and let it rip.
cards.why: Free stand-up from a headliner-level comic is a genuinely rare Thursday night.

cards.name: Spark60: Meditations for Mortals: The Liberating Art of Embracing Your Limits with Oliver Burkeman
cards.blurb: Catch Oliver Burkeman's talk on embracing limits and getting more done by doing less — it's a genuinely interesting angle on productivity and mortality that'll have you thinking all day.
cards.why: Free talk, sharp ideas, and a good way to kick off a Thursday morning.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Walk through the Triton's current contemporary exhibitions and spend some time in the sculpture garden outside. It's genuinely world-class work in a low-key setting that never feels crowded.
cards.why: Free admission and you're already in Santa Clara, so it's a natural next stop.

cards.name: Hungry Hound
cards.address: 1000 Lafayette St C, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Grab lunch at Hungry Hound before the afternoon fills up. It's a straightforward, well-rated spot with prices that won't hurt.
cards.why: Under $15 and rated 4.5 — hard to beat for a quick, solid lunch.

cards.name: AI-Powered tools to help with your searching
cards.blurb: Sit in on this free session on AI-powered search tools. Whether you use it for work or personal research, you'll walk out with at least a couple of tricks you didn't have before.
cards.why: It's free, practical, and you'll actually use what you learn.

cards.name: San Jose Municipal Rose Garden
cards.address: Municipal Rose Garden
cards.blurb: Take a slow walk through the rose garden while the afternoon light is still good. April is prime blooming season so the color and fragrance are at their peak right now.
cards.why: Perfect spring afternoon for it, and it costs nothing.

cards.name: Palermo Italian Restaurant
cards.address: 791 Auzerais Ave, San Jose, CA 95126, USA
cards.blurb: Finish the night with dinner at Palermo. Order whatever the kitchen is pushing and lean into the Italian comfort food in a low-key San Jose neighborhood spot.
cards.why: Solid 4.5 rating, reasonable prices, and a relaxed vibe to close out the day.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Walk through the galleries and spend real time in the sculpture garden outside. The contemporary art here holds up and the free admission makes it a no-brainer.
cards.why: You're already in the arts headspace from Burkeman's talk, so keep that energy going somewhere beautiful.

cards.name: Taplands
cards.address: 1171 Homestead Rd, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Grab a seat at Taplands and pick through their rotating tap list over lunch. Great spot to decompress with a pint after a full morning.
cards.why: It's a solid local spot in Santa Clara and a perfect midday reset.

cards.name: Natalie and James Thompson Gallery: Click on My Mouth: Language and Technology at ART 127: Thompson Gallery
cards.blurb: Check out the Click on My Mouth exhibit at Thompson Gallery, where the show digs into the intersection of language and technology through contemporary art.
cards.why: It ties right into the themes from this morning and it's free to walk in.

cards.name: Philz Coffee
cards.address: 118 Paseo de San Antonio, San Jose, CA 95112, USA
cards.blurb: Stop at Philz for an afternoon cup before the evening kicks off. Good call on a day this full.
cards.why: You'll want the recharge before dinner and it closes at 8 so the timing works perfectly.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Hit San Pedro Square Market for dinner and graze through the vendor stalls. Mix and match from tacos, banh mi, ramen, whatever sounds good, and grab a patio seat if the evening warms up.
cards.why: No better way to end a full day than grazing through a dozen options with no pressure to commit to one thing.

cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Walk the creek trail and let the morning shake off. It's shaded, flat, and genuinely pretty along the water.
cards.why: 54°F now and climbing to 74°F makes this the sweet spot for a morning walk.

cards.name: Trattoria 360
cards.address: 360 E Campbell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Swing through Vasona Lake on your way out, grab a bench by the water, and decompress before lunch.
cards.why: It's a genuinely relaxing spot on a clear spring day.

cards.name: Recycle Bookstore West
cards.address: 275 E Campbell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Browse the shelves at Recycle Bookstore West on Campbell Ave. Used books, good prices, and the kind of place you lose an hour in without noticing.
cards.why: Great way to wind down after a walk without committing to anything intense.

cards.name: Bayon Temple
cards.address: 2425 S Bascom Ave suite a, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Lunch at Bayon Temple. It's a Cambodian spot on Bascom with a strong local following. Try the local favorite dishes and take your time with it.
cards.why: Solid 4.6 rating and a cuisine you don't see everywhere.

cards.name: Craft Club for Adults: The Joy of Drawing - Tulips in Spring
cards.blurb: Head to the Saratoga library for the drop-in drawing class focused on tulips. No experience needed, just show up and draw.
cards.why: Free, creative, and a nice afternoon change of pace.

cards.name: Body Rest Massage Wellness Center
cards.address: 621 E Campbell Ave #11e, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Book a session at Body Rest Massage Wellness Center on Campbell Ave. Let someone else take care of you for an hour after a full morning out.
cards.why: 4.8 rating and open until 9 PM, so you've got plenty of flexibility.

cards.name: Forbes Mill Steakhouse
cards.address: 206 N Santa Cruz Ave, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Dinner at Forbes Mill Steakhouse on North Santa Cruz. This is a proper splurge night. Go with the steak and don't skip dessert.
cards.why: It's a Thursday night and you deserve a real dinner out.

cards.name: Horizon Bakery
cards.address: 19929 Stevens Creek Blvd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Swing by Horizon Bakery for lunch. Grab a pastry, a savory bite, and something to sip while you settle in at a table.
cards.why: It's right in Cupertino and rated 4.6 for a reason — solid spot to refuel after qigong.

cards.name: Stevens Canyon Trail
cards.address: 13492 Stevens Canyon Rd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Hit Stevens Canyon Trail for a few hours of foothill hiking. The trail runs along the creek with good tree cover and enough elevation to feel like you actually went somewhere.
cards.why: 72°F and sunny is basically perfect hiking weather for this time of year.

cards.name: Main Street Cupertino
cards.address: 19401 Stevens Creek Blvd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Wander Main Street Cupertino and browse the outdoor plaza. Good for window shopping, people-watching, and grabbing a coffee or snack before the evening.
cards.why: The outdoor vibe is easy and relaxed, and there's plenty to poke around without any pressure.

cards.name: Astounding Massage Management
cards.address: 4155 Moorpark Ave, San Jose, CA 95117, USA
cards.blurb: Book a slot at Astounding Massage Management in San Jose for a proper evening massage. Rated 4.9, so expectations are high and apparently met.
cards.why: After a hiking day, this is exactly what your legs are asking for.

cards.name: Thursday Evening Meditation and Mindfulness
cards.blurb: Cap the night at the Thursday Evening Meditation and Mindfulness session at Cupertino Library. Show up, breathe, decompress.
cards.why: Free, mellow, and a genuinely good way to close out a Thursday.

cards.name: Hummus Mediterranean Kitchen
cards.address: 715 Stanford Shopping Center, Palo Alto, CA 94304, USA
cards.blurb: Kick off the evening with a solid Mediterranean spread at Hummus Mediterranean Kitchen inside Stanford Shopping Center.
cards.why: It's a relaxed, satisfying dinner before the night picks up.

cards.name: Color Me Mine
cards.address: 321 University Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Swing into Color Me Mine on University Ave and browse the studio while you wait to head to your next stop.
cards.why: Gets you in a creative headspace right before the drawing session.

cards.name: Graphic Novel Book Club
cards.blurb: Grab a seat at the Graphic Novel Book Club and dig into whatever book is on the table tonight.
cards.why: Free, low-key, and a fun way to meet other people who actually read.

cards.name: Sundance The Steakhouse
cards.address: 1921 El Camino Real, Palo Alto, CA 94306, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down the night at Sundance The Steakhouse with a proper late dinner, think a good cut and a glass of something worth savoring.
cards.why: It's a classic Palo Alto spot that earns its reputation every time.

cards.name: SaiGon BreadFast
cards.address: 123 E El Camino Real, Mountain View, CA 94040, USA
cards.blurb: Hit SaiGon BreadFast early for a banh mi and some coffee before the day gets going.
cards.why: 4.6 stars and a 7 PM close means it works perfectly for a morning start.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Spend a couple hours at the Computer History Museum working through the exhibits on how we got from punch cards to pocket supercomputers.
cards.why: It's genuinely fascinating stuff even if you're not a tech person, and $20 gets you a solid two hours.

cards.name: La Avenida Bike Trail Head
cards.address: 1003-1001 La Avenida St, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Take the La Avenida Bike Trail for a midday stretch along the bay path, great for a walk or a ride if you brought wheels.
cards.why: 71 degrees and 5% rain chance is basically perfect trail weather.

cards.name: Bloomsgiving
cards.address: 301 Castro St, Mountain View, CA 94041, USA
cards.blurb: Grab lunch at Bloomsgiving on Castro St, a solid local spot to refuel before the afternoon.
cards.why: 4.6 stars and open until 8 PM, it fits cleanly into the early afternoon slot.

cards.name: Next Level Board Game Cafe
cards.address: 888 Villa St, Mountain View, CA 94041, USA
cards.blurb: Wrap the night at Next Level Board Game Cafe. Grab dinner, pick a game from their library, and settle in for a few rounds.
cards.why: 4.9 rating and open until 10 PM makes it the best possible Thursday night closer.

cards.name: Izzy's Brooklyn Bagels
cards.address: 477 California Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94306, USA
cards.blurb: Start the day with a bagel and coffee at this California Ave staple — order a classic everything bagel with lox and settle in.
cards.why: It's a proper breakfast spot and closes at 3 PM so morning is when it shines.

cards.name: The Dish Trail
cards.address: Stanford Foothills (Stanford Ave trailhead)
cards.blurb: Hit the Stanford Ave trailhead and take the loop up to The Dish — the views of the Bay from the top are worth the climb, and the 72°F high means it'll be comfortable by the time you're heading back down.
cards.why: Perfect morning weather for this iconic hike before the day heats up.

cards.name: Cantor Arts Center
cards.address: Cantor Arts Center, Stanford University
cards.blurb: Cool off at the Cantor Arts Center on Stanford's campus. Walk the Rodin sculpture garden outside, then head in for a spin through the galleries — free and genuinely world-class.
cards.why: It's free, it's on campus, and it's a great way to shift gears after the hike.

cards.name: Evvia Estiatorio
cards.address: 420 Emerson St, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Evvia for a late Greek lunch. Order the lamb chops or whatever the mezze spread looks like today — this is a proper sit-down meal worth lingering over.
cards.why: One of the best restaurants in Palo Alto and worth every dollar at the $30-60 range.

cards.name: Drop-In Drawing @ The Anderson
cards.address: 314 Lomita Dr, Stanford, CA 94305
cards.blurb: Drop into the Anderson Collection for free drawing time — bring your own sketchbook or just show up and work from the art around you.
cards.why: It's a free, low-key creative afternoon and a great way to spend a couple hours at Stanford.

cards.name: Watercourse Way Bath House Spa
cards.address: 165 Channing Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Book a private soaking tub at Watercourse Way and decompress for an hour or two. It's a quintessential Palo Alto move and the perfect way to wind down your body after the hike.
cards.why: Open until 11 PM and genuinely restorative after an active day.

cards.name: Graphic Novel Book Club
cards.blurb: Wrap the night at the Graphic Novel Book Club. Even if you haven't read whatever they're discussing, these things are usually a great crowd and a fun conversation.
cards.why: It's free, it's tonight, and it's a nice social cap to a full day.

cards.name: Friends of The Library Membership Month Book Sale - Friends Preview Sale (w/ Annual Meeting)
cards.blurb: Hit the Friends Preview Sale before it opens to the public — dig through the tables for cheap paperbacks, cookbooks, and random gems you didn't know you needed.
cards.why: Free, low-key, and genuinely fun to browse if you like books.

cards.name: Philz Coffee
cards.address: 125 S Frances St, Sunnyvale, CA 94086, USA
cards.blurb: Grab a slow-drip Philz blend and decompress before dinner. The Pour Over is the move.
cards.why: Good way to wind down and ease into the evening.

cards.name: Spark60: Meditations for Mortals: The Liberating Art of Embracing Your Limits with Oliver Burkeman
cards.blurb: Oliver Burkeman wrote one of the more genuinely useful productivity books in years. This talk is about ditching the guilt of not doing it all and actually enjoying the finite time you have.
cards.why: Free talk that's actually worth your Thursday night.

cards.name: Urban Plates
cards.address: 300 W McKinley Ave Suite 100, Sunnyvale, CA 94086, USA
cards.blurb: Urban Plates does made-from-scratch comfort food in a laid-back setting. Build a plate around whatever looks good on the board tonight.
cards.why: Solid food without a long wait or a big bill.

cards.name: Spark60: Meditations for Mortals: The Liberating Art of Embracing Your Limits with Oliver Burkeman
cards.blurb: Catch Oliver Burkeman live as he talks through his ideas on limits, mortality, and why doing less might actually be the point — it's a genuinely thought-provoking way to kick off the morning.
cards.why: Free, thought-provoking, and it starts right now — hard to beat that for a Thursday morning.

cards.name: Dr.ink
cards.address: 77 N Almaden Ave #70, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Stop in for a drink before the market — grab a coffee, a juice, or something a little more creative to carry into the morning.
cards.why: Great ratings and right in downtown San Jose, perfect timing before your 11 AM stop.

cards.name: Fists of Fury at King Library
cards.blurb: Swing by Swana Sweets at the Student Union and grab something sweet to snack on while you check out the Fists of Fury exhibit at King Library, an outdoor display on the history of martial arts and resistance.
cards.why: Both are free, both are on campus, and it makes for a fun low-key midmorning stretch.

cards.name: Mio Vicino
cards.address: 1290 Benton St, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Head over to Mio Vicino for a proper sit-down lunch — solid Italian in a neighborhood setting, the kind of place where you actually linger over your food.
cards.why: You've earned a real meal after a full morning, and the ratings here back it up.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Walk through the Triton Museum of Art and spend time in both the gallery and the sculpture garden out back — the contemporary exhibitions rotate frequently and the outdoor space is genuinely nice on a day like this.
cards.why: Free admission, great art, and a sculpture garden you can actually walk around in the afternoon light.

cards.name: Chicago Presidential Reception
cards.blurb: Head over to SCU's Chicago Presidential Reception — mingle with the crowd and soak in whatever's going on with this campus event, it's free and worth seeing what the university puts together.
cards.why: Free, on campus, and a genuinely interesting way to spend late afternoon.

cards.name: Salt & Straw
cards.address: 2855 Stevens Creek Blvd, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: End the day with a scoop (or two) at Salt and Straw on Stevens Creek. Their flavors are inventive and seasonal, and the lines move fast enough that you're in and out with ice cream in hand before long.
cards.why: It's a perfect low-key closer after a full day, and the 72-degree high makes evening ice cream a no-brainer.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Walk the sculpture garden first, then take your time with the contemporary galleries inside. The Triton punches way above its weight for a free museum.
cards.why: It's a perfect follow-up to a talk about art and limits — and free world-class art on a Thursday morning is hard to beat.

cards.name: Paesano Ristorante Italiano
cards.address: 350 W Julian St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Grab a table at Paesano and work through their Italian lunch menu. Solid pasta, good atmosphere, reasonable prices for a sit-down spot in downtown San Jose.
cards.why: You've earned a proper lunch after a full morning of arts and culture.

cards.name: Join Zsolt Jozsef Simon — Porcelain Casting Workshop at Art & Design Building
cards.blurb: Drop into the Porcelain Casting Workshop at SJSU's Art and Design Building. You'll actually get your hands dirty learning the casting process alongside students — zero experience needed.
cards.why: It's free, it's hands-on, and it keeps the creative energy from this morning going strong.

cards.name: Live Oak Park
cards.address: 641 Moreland Way, Santa Clara, CA 95054, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down with a stop at A Twist of Lime before they close. Catch a quick drink or snack and decompress after a full afternoon of activity.
cards.why: A 5-star rating in Santa Clara is rare. Worth seeing what the fuss is about.

cards.name: Trattoria 360
cards.address: 360 E Campbell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Trattoria 360 for dinner on Campbell Ave. The rating is 4.7 for a reason. Order pasta, split a bottle of wine, and let the evening stretch out.
cards.why: It's the highest-rated restaurant in the pool and a great way to close out a genuinely full Thursday.

cards.name: Japantown San Jose
cards.address: Japantown, San Jose
cards.blurb: Start the morning at Okayama Kobo for fresh mochi and pastries, then wander Jackson St and browse the Japanese grocery before the day gets busy.
cards.why: Best low-key morning walk in San Jose, and the mochi alone is worth the trip.

cards.name: State of Illusion Exhibition
cards.blurb: Check out the State of Illusion Exhibition in Santa Clara — the kind of art show that messes with your perception in the best way.
cards.why: Free, thought-provoking, and a solid way to spend a Thursday morning.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Grab lunch at San Pedro Square Market and graze your way through the vendor stalls. Tacos, banh mi, ramen — pick two and find a patio seat.
cards.why: You won't find a better lunch setup downtown when the weather's this nice.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Swing through the Triton Museum of Art and spend time in the sculpture garden before heading out. The contemporary galleries are genuinely worth a slow walk.
cards.why: Free world-class art and it closes at 4, so the timing works out perfectly today.

cards.name: In-Person English Language Learner (ELL) Conversation Club
cards.blurb: Drop into the In-Person English Language Learner Conversation Club at the library — great for meeting people and practicing casual conversation in a relaxed setting.
cards.why: Free, social, and a fun way to spend an hour before dinner.

cards.name: Home Eat汉家宴 - San Jose
cards.address: 88 S 4th St STE 150, San Jose, CA 95112, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Home Eat 汉家宴 for dinner. It's a 4.8-rated Chinese spot in downtown San Jose, so order whatever the table recommends and settle in.
cards.why: A 4.8 rating means people are serious about this place.

cards.name: EDscapade Games
cards.address: 1150 Murphy Ave #200, San Jose, CA 95131, USA
cards.blurb: Wrap the night at EDscapade Games for an escape room. Book a room ahead if you can, then see how fast you can get out.
cards.why: Nothing kills a Thursday night faster than sitting at home when you could be solving puzzles.

cards.name: Christy Donuts
cards.address: 1291 E Calaveras Blvd, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Start the day at Christy Donuts — grab a couple of their famous donuts and coffee before the morning gets going.
cards.why: A Milpitas staple with a loyal following and a 4.6 rating for good reason.

cards.name: McCandless Park
cards.address: 1576 McCandless Dr, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Take a relaxed walk through McCandless Park, one of the nicest spots in Milpitas to just stretch your legs and enjoy the morning air.
cards.why: Perfect weather to be outside before you settle in for the late morning.

cards.name: Qi Gong Meditation & Exercise Class at Milpitas Library
cards.blurb: Join the Mend with Friends group at the Milpitas Library for a relaxed social crafting session.
cards.why: Low-key and social, a great way to spend a Thursday morning with your people.

cards.name: Saucy Asian - Milpitas
cards.address: Food Court, 447 Great Mall Dr Space 10, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Head over to Saucy Asian in the Great Mall food court for lunch. A 4.7 rating speaks for itself — try whatever's looking good on the menu.
cards.why: One of the highest-rated spots in Milpitas and a solid late lunch stop.

cards.name: Qi Gong Meditation & Exercise Class at Milpitas Library
cards.blurb: Stay at the library for Qi Gong Meditation and Exercise Class. It's free, it's mellow, and you'll actually feel good after.
cards.why: Back-to-back library events means you're already there — no reason not to stick around.

cards.name: Intel Museum
cards.address: 2200 Mission College Blvd, Santa Clara, CA 95054, USA
cards.blurb: Swing by the Intel Museum in Santa Clara for a surprisingly cool look at the history of Silicon Valley's most iconic chip maker.
cards.why: Free to visit and genuinely interesting even if tech isn't your thing.

cards.name: Oklava Cafe
cards.address: 205 University Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at Oklava Cafe on University Ave. Grab a coffee and something from the menu to ease into the day at a solid pace.
cards.why: Great ratings and a central Palo Alto spot to kick things off right.

cards.name: Cantor Arts Center
cards.address: Cantor Arts Center, Stanford University
cards.blurb: Walk the Cantor Arts Center on Stanford campus. Spend time with the Rodin sculpture garden outside and whatever's on in the galleries inside.
cards.why: Free world-class art on a Thursday morning with basically no crowds.

cards.name: Spotlight Tours Thursdays | Anderson Collection
cards.address: 314 Lomita Dr, Stanford, CA 94305
cards.blurb: Catch one of the Spotlight Tours Thursdays at the Anderson Collection, right on campus. A guided look at the collection beats wandering alone.
cards.why: It's free, it's today, and it sets you up perfectly for the Drop-In Drawing session later.

cards.name: Avy Spa Massage
cards.address: 3783 El Camino Real, Palo Alto, CA 94306, USA
cards.blurb: Book an afternoon slot at Avy Spa Massage and let someone fix whatever the week did to your shoulders.
cards.why: A 4.9 rating doesn't lie, and it's wide open this afternoon.

cards.name: Pelicans, Herons, and Egrets, Oh My!
cards.blurb: Wrap the evening at the Pelicans, Herons, and Egrets talk in Los Altos. A free evening program about local birds, which sounds low-key but always ends up being a great time.
cards.why: Free, unusual, and a perfect Thursday night wind-down after a full day.

cards.name: Manresa Bread
cards.address: 271 State St, Los Altos, CA 94022, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at Manresa Bread, one of the best bakeries in the South Bay. Grab a pastry and coffee and ease into the day.
cards.why: Hard to beat a Thursday morning here when the pastry case is fresh.

cards.name: Cantor Arts Center
cards.address: Cantor Arts Center, Stanford University
cards.blurb: Wander the Cantor Arts Center on Stanford's campus. The Rodin sculpture garden outside is worth the trip on its own, and the galleries inside are genuinely world-class.
cards.why: Free, beautiful, and a great way to spend a cool morning before it warms up.

cards.name: Spotlight Tours Thursdays | Anderson Collection
cards.address: 314 Lomita Dr, Stanford, CA 94305
cards.blurb: Catch one of the Spotlight Tours at the Anderson Collection. These focused guided tours dig into specific works in the collection and give you a real read on the art.
cards.why: It's free and the Anderson is one of the best modern art collections on the West Coast.

cards.name: Ethel's Fancy
cards.address: 550 Waverley St, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Ethel's Fancy on Waverley for lunch. Order whatever's seasonal and grab a table if one opens up.
cards.why: It's a local favorite and exactly the kind of spot you want for a relaxed weekday lunch.

cards.name: The Dish Trail
cards.address: Stanford Foothills (Stanford Ave trailhead)
cards.blurb: Hike The Dish Trail up into the Stanford foothills. The views from the top are some of the best in the South Bay, and 72°F is perfect hiking weather.
cards.why: Perfect weather for it and you'll have worked up an appetite by the time the game rolls around.

cards.name: Topi Cakes
cards.address: 2305 El Camino Real B, Palo Alto, CA 94306, USA
cards.blurb: Quick stop at Topi Cakes for something sweet before the game. Grab a treat to go.
cards.why: Best pre-game dessert detour you'll find.

cards.name: UMe | Tea & Snacks
cards.address: 220 Castro St, Mountain View, CA 94041, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at this cozy Castro St tea and snacks spot — grab something warm and ease into the day before heading to your first stop.
cards.why: Good ratings, low cost, and the perfect low-key way to kick off a Thursday morning.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Swing through the Computer History Museum and let yourself get genuinely sucked in. The exhibits on early computing, AI, and the wild personalities behind Silicon Valley are way more interesting than the name suggests.
cards.why: It's right there in Mountain View and the history here is legitimately wild — you'll leave having learned something.

cards.name: Shoreline Park & Lake
cards.address: Shoreline at Mountain View
cards.blurb: Head over to Shoreline Park for a walk along the water and some fresh air. The Bay views are wide open and it's a great way to stretch out after a morning of sitting.
cards.why: 71°F high and only 5% rain chance makes this a genuinely nice afternoon outside.

cards.name: State of Mind Public House and Pizzeria
cards.address: 101 Plaza N, Los Altos, CA 94022, USA
cards.blurb: Head to State of Mind Public House and Pizzeria for a late lunch. Get a pizza and a beer and take your time with it.
cards.why: 4.6 stars and solid midrange prices make this a great afternoon wind-down meal.

cards.name: South Bay Ceramics - Studio 1954
cards.address: 1954 Old Middlefield Way STE I, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Drop into South Bay Ceramics Studio 1954 and see what's happening — whether it's an open studio session or just a browse, it's a genuinely fun creative stop.
cards.why: A hands-on activity in the early evening is a nice change of pace from sightseeing.

cards.name: Mother Goose Time
cards.blurb: Cap the night at the Friends of the Library Book Sale preview in Mountain View. Dig through the stacks and pick up a few things you didn't know you needed.
cards.why: Free entry and it starts at 5 PM, so catching it in the evening slots in perfectly after dinner.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning walking the sculpture garden, then head inside to browse the contemporary exhibitions — it's free, unhurried, and genuinely impressive for a Thursday morning.
cards.why: Cool enough outside that the sculpture garden feels great, and you've got the place basically to yourself on a weekday.

cards.name: Jessica Monette: Root Me in the Soil, In the Wake and the Becoming (Project Room Exhibition)
cards.blurb: Swing by the SCU Project Room to check out Jessica Monette's current exhibition, a visually striking show about roots, land, and transformation.
cards.why: Free art, open today, and a nice continuation of the creative morning.

cards.name: Mikiya Wagyu Shabu House | Santa Clara
cards.address: 3590 Homestead Rd, Santa Clara, CA 95051, USA
cards.blurb: Hit up Mikiya Wagyu Shabu House for a shabu-shabu lunch — you pick your broth, load up the pot with wagyu, and go at your own pace.
cards.why: Wagyu shabu at lunch feels indulgent in the best way possible.

cards.name: AI-Powered tools to help with your searching
cards.blurb: Catch this free session on AI-powered search tools — practical, current, and the kind of thing you'll actually use.
cards.why: It's free and useful, which is a hard combo to beat on a Thursday afternoon.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Spend the rest of the afternoon at the Computer History Museum, wandering through decades of tech history from punch cards to the internet — it's way more interesting than it sounds.
cards.why: The breadth of what's in here is genuinely wild, and you'll lose track of time.

cards.name: Gourmet Works
cards.address: 334 Main St, Los Altos, CA 94022, USA
cards.blurb: Browse Gourmet Works on Main St in Los Altos before it closes at 6 — great spot to poke around for kitchen finds, specialty ingredients, or a fun gift.
cards.why: A nice low-key wind-down before dinner.

cards.name: Dumont Creamery & Café
cards.address: 28 N Almaden Ave Suite 40, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning with coffee and something good to eat at this highly-rated downtown cafe before the day kicks off.
cards.why: A 4.7-rated cafe is the right way to ease into a Thursday.

cards.name: Spark60: Meditations for Mortals: The Liberating Art of Embracing Your Limits with Oliver Burkeman
cards.blurb: Catch Oliver Burkeman talking about embracing limits and finding freedom in what you can't control — it's the kind of talk that sticks with you.
cards.why: Free, thought-provoking, and it's happening right now this morning.

cards.name: Black Corporate Board Readiness Program (BCBR C16) In Session
cards.blurb: Pick up something sweet from Swana Sweets at the Student Union — browse the outdoor setup and see what's on offer today.
cards.why: It's a locked stop and a perfect midday treat between activities.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Spend a couple hours at the Triton Museum of Art, walking the sculpture garden and checking out the contemporary exhibitions inside.
cards.why: Free, genuinely impressive, and most people in the South Bay still haven't been.

cards.name: Little Wine House
cards.address: 350 W Julian St Bldg 4, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down at Little Wine House with a glass or two in a laid-back setting before the evening picks up.
cards.why: A perfect-rated wine spot is a solid Thursday evening move.

cards.name: Anthony Jeselnik
cards.address: 62 S Second St
cards.blurb: Anthony Jeselnik is performing live tonight at 62 S Second St. Get there a little early, grab a spot, and enjoy a sharp stand-up set.
cards.why: Great comedy, free, and a fun way to close out a solid Thursday.

cards.name: Bakenstein
cards.address: 1370 Homestead Rd, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Swing by Bakenstein for a late morning bite — rated 4.9 for a reason, so just order whatever looks good and don't overthink it.
cards.why: It's the highest-rated spot in the pool and it's right there in Santa Clara.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Wander the Triton Museum of Art at your own pace — the contemporary galleries are genuinely worth slowing down for, and the sculpture garden is a nice way to wrap it up before the afternoon crowd arrives.
cards.why: Free, no kids, good art. Hard to argue with that on a 70-degree Thursday.

cards.name: Swana Sweets at Student Union
cards.blurb: Check out Swana Sweets at the SJSU Student Union — a free outdoor event worth strolling through for a bit.
cards.why: It's free and happening today, so why not pop by.

cards.name: R&R, 2026: Recovering Ricardo Favela’s Royal Chicano Air Force Poster Collection at San José State University at King Library
cards.blurb: Head over to King Library and spend some time with Ricardo Favela's Royal Chicano Air Force poster collection — this is the kind of local art history that doesn't get enough attention.
cards.why: Free, no time pressure, and genuinely interesting if you're already in the arts headspace from this morning.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Hit San Pedro Square Market for dinner and pick your way through the vendor stalls — tacos, banh mi, ramen, whatever sounds good. Grab a patio seat if the evening stays warm.
cards.why: Mix-and-match dinner with no commitment to one cuisine is exactly right for a Thursday night.

cards.name: Petroglyph Ceramic Lounge
cards.address: 1140 Lincoln Ave, San Jose, CA 95125, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down the night at Petroglyph Ceramic Lounge — paint a piece, zone out, and have a genuinely relaxing couple of hours with no agenda.
cards.why: After a full day of thinking and walking, making something with your hands is a solid way to finish.

cards.name: Kovai Cafe - Milpitas
cards.address: 181 Ranch Dr, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at Kovai Cafe, a highly rated South Indian spot — grab a dosa or some chai and ease into the day.
cards.why: A 4.8 rating in Milpitas means locals know something you should too.

cards.name: Sunnyhills Albert Augustine Jr. Memorial Park
cards.address: Cortez St &, Coelho St, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Take a quick stroll through Sunnyhills Albert Augustine Jr. Memorial Park to stretch your legs before the meetup.
cards.why: Perfect morning weather for a short walk before you head inside.

cards.name: Qi Gong Meditation & Exercise Class at Milpitas Library
cards.blurb: Mend with Friends — bring whatever needs fixing and hang out with folks doing the same.
cards.why: It's a low-key, good-vibes kind of morning activity.

cards.name: Poki Run
cards.address: 119 N Milpitas Blvd, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Poki Run for a build-your-own poke bowl lunch — fresh, fast, and easy on the wallet.
cards.why: Under $15 and a 4.6 rating makes this a no-brainer afternoon meal.

cards.name: Qi Gong Meditation & Exercise Class at Milpitas Library
cards.blurb: Stick around the library for the Qi Gong Meditation and Exercise Class — free, gentle movement, no experience needed.
cards.why: It's free and a genuinely nice way to reset after the morning.

cards.name: Strikers Cricket
cards.address: 1453 N Milpitas Blvd, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Strikers Cricket for some batting practice or just watch a game — this is one of the highest-rated spots in Milpitas for a reason.
cards.why: A 4.9 rating is almost unheard of and it's a genuinely fun afternoon out.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Spend the morning walking through decades of computing history, from room-sized mainframes to the first personal computers. The exhibits are genuinely wild even if you're not a tech person.
cards.why: It opens early and gives you a solid two hours of interesting stuff before the day kicks off.

cards.name: SWEET55 - Swiss Chocolates & Confections
cards.address: 855 El Camino Real Suite 123, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Grab a box of Swiss chocolates or confections to snack on. The selection is small but seriously good, and it makes for a sweet mid-morning pick-me-up before lunch.
cards.why: A quick, indulgent stop that's worth every calorie.

cards.name: Cantor Arts Center
cards.address: Cantor Arts Center, Stanford University
cards.blurb: Walk the Rodin Sculpture Garden outside, then head in to browse the permanent collection. It's free, it's on Stanford's campus, and the quality of the art here will catch you off guard.
cards.why: Free world-class art on a beautiful Thursday afternoon is hard to beat.

cards.name: Anton
cards.address: 660 Stanford Shopping Center, Palo Alto, CA 94304, USA
cards.blurb: Settle in for lunch at Anton inside Stanford Shopping Center. Try whatever the kitchen is featuring and take your time with it.
cards.why: Rated 4.7 and a legitimately great lunch spot in Palo Alto.

cards.name: Spotlight Tours Thursdays | Anderson Collection
cards.address: 314 Lomita Dr, Stanford, CA 94305
cards.blurb: Drop in for the Thursday Spotlight Tour at the Anderson Collection, just steps from Cantor. You'll get a guided look at some of the best postwar and contemporary American art around.
cards.why: Free, no signup needed, and it's a genuinely great way to spend an hour on campus.

cards.name: Massage Therapy Center Palo Alto
cards.address: 368 California Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94306, USA
cards.blurb: Book a massage or bodywork session at Massage Therapy Center Palo Alto on California Ave. A solid way to decompress after a full afternoon on your feet.
cards.why: Rated 4.8 and open until 9, so you can slide right in after the Anderson.

cards.name: Graphic Novel Book Club
cards.blurb: Head to the Graphic Novel Book Club at the Palo Alto library. Even if you haven't read the pick, these tend to be loose, fun discussions where showing up curious is enough.
cards.why: Free, low-key, and a fun way to end the night with some conversation.

cards.name: Goodthing coffee
cards.address: 532 Ramona St, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at Goodthing coffee on Ramona — grab a coffee and settle in before the day kicks off.
cards.why: It's a great Palo Alto coffee spot and the perfect low-key way to ease into a Thursday.

cards.name: Drop-In Drawing @ The Anderson
cards.address: 314 Lomita Dr, Stanford, CA 94305
cards.blurb: Drop into the Anderson Collection's drawing session and actually make something — no experience needed, just show up and sketch.
cards.why: Free, creative, and a genuinely fun way to spend a morning at one of Stanford's best spaces.

cards.name: Spotlight Tours Thursdays | Anderson Collection
cards.address: 314 Lomita Dr, Stanford, CA 94305
cards.blurb: Catch a Spotlight Tour at the Anderson Collection right after drawing — a docent walks you through the highlights of the collection with real context and stories.
cards.why: You're already there, and these tours make the art actually click.

cards.name: Shoreline Park
cards.address: 3160 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Shoreline Park for a walk around the lake, some fresh air, and a look at the Bay — the windsurfers are usually out and it never gets old.
cards.why: It's going to be close to 70 by afternoon and this is one of the best places in the South Bay to enjoy it.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Spend a couple hours at the Computer History Museum going through the exhibits — the timeline of computing from room-sized machines to smartphones is genuinely wild to walk through.
cards.why: Even if you're not a tech person, the scale of what's in here is hard not to geek out over.

cards.name: Simply Sabor
cards.address: 340 Castro St, Mountain View, CA 94041, USA
cards.blurb: Quick dinner at Simply Sabor on Castro Street before the game — get the tacos, share a few things, and keep it casual.
cards.why: A 4.8-rated spot that won't slow you down before first pitch.

cards.name: 2026 Development and Political Economics PhD Student Conference (DevPEC)
cards.address: 366 Galvez Street, Stanford, CA 94305
cards.blurb: Stanford Cardinal vs. Santa Clara Broncos at the baseball stadium. Grab a seat, soak in the evening air, and enjoy a live college game.
cards.why: College baseball on a warm spring evening is hard to beat.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Get there right when it opens and spend an hour wandering the exhibits before your 10 AM commitment kicks in. The history of computing is genuinely wild even if you're not a tech person.
cards.why: It's a great way to ease into the morning with something interesting.

cards.name: Palmetto Superfoods - Mountain View
cards.address: 2565 California St Suite 83, Mountain View, CA 94040, USA
cards.blurb: Swing by Palmetto Superfoods for a fresh, feel-good lunch. Great spot to refuel with something lighter after a busy morning.
cards.why: High-rated local spot that won't weigh you down for the afternoon.

cards.name: Shoreline Park & Lake
cards.address: Shoreline at Mountain View
cards.blurb: Head out for a walk or bike ride along the water. The bay views are wide open and the 71-degree afternoon is going to feel perfect out here.
cards.why: Perfect weather for it and it's one of the better outdoor spots in the area.

cards.name: Friends of the Mountain View Library
cards.address: 585 Franklin St, Mountain View, CA 94041, USA
cards.blurb: Browse the shelves at Friends of the Mountain View Library. You never know what you'll find here, and prices are basically nothing.
cards.why: A fun low-key browse that pairs well with the library event happening later.

cards.name: Rootstock Wine Bar
cards.address: 331 Castro St, Mountain View, CA 94041, USA
cards.blurb: Grab a glass of wine and settle in at Rootstock on Castro Street. It's a relaxed spot to wind down before the evening event kicks off.
cards.why: Great wine list and an easy vibe that makes it hard to leave.

cards.name: Friends of The Library Membership Month Book Sale - Friends Preview Sale (w/ Annual Meeting)
cards.blurb: Head over to the Friends Preview Sale and Annual Meeting. Free entry, book deals, and the kind of crowd that actually reads.
cards.why: It's free and it's today, so you might as well see what's on the shelves.

cards.name: Arwa Yemeni Coffee
cards.address: 605 Tasman Dr, Sunnyvale, CA 94089, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning with a cup of Yemeni coffee — rich, cardamom-forward, and nothing like your usual drip.
cards.why: Best way to kick off a Thursday morning in Sunnyvale.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Walk through the full history of computing, from room-sized mainframes to the first personal computers. The exhibits are genuinely wild even if you're not a tech person.
cards.why: A $20 ticket for two hours of stuff that'll actually surprise you.

cards.name: Rancho San Antonio County Park & Open Space Preserve
cards.address: Cupertino, CA, USA
cards.blurb: Grab lunch at Rancho San Antonio after the museum — hike the flat Permanente Creek Trail loop or push up to the farm for views of the valley.
cards.why: Perfect conditions for a midday walk with 71°F and almost no rain.

cards.name: Kurated Kitchens
cards.address: 155 E Maude Ave, Sunnyvale, CA 94085, USA
cards.blurb: Kurated Kitchens is a shared culinary space in Sunnyvale worth checking out for a solid midday meal with an interesting rotating concept.
cards.why: The 4.6 rating says it all, and it closes at 4 so get in while you can.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Wander through the Triton Museum's galleries and step outside into the sculpture garden while the light is still good.
cards.why: Free, beautiful, and way more impressive than people expect for a local museum.

cards.name: Pelicans, Herons, and Egrets, Oh My!
cards.blurb: Catch this free talk on pelicans, herons, and egrets in Los Altos tonight. It's the kind of low-key, genuinely interesting local event that makes a weeknight feel like something.
cards.why: Free, fun, and a totally different vibe for a Thursday night.
